steps.title: Spot the required evidence
steps.content: The claim that 'Aha Pūnana Leo increased young speakers of Hawaiian requires direct proof: concrete numbers showing growth in young Hawaiian speakers.

steps.title: Match the data to the claim
steps.content: Choice B provides concrete evidence: fewer than fifty children could speak Hawaiian when 'Aha Pūnana Leo was founded, but now more than 2,000 students at the school speak it. This directly shows the increase in young Hawaiian speakers that the claim describes. The other choices don't address growth in young speakers—they discuss other communities, linguistic similarities, and population statistics. This matches choice B — B is correct.
